task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2213: [Feature] Bifrost Gateway Deployment on EC2 with Cloudflare Tunnel ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2213` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2213 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: [Feature] Bifrost Gateway Deployment on EC2 with Cloudflare Tunnel (#2213)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2213` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2213" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: [Feature] Bifrost Gateway Deployment on EC2 with Cloudflare Tunnel" body: "Closes #2213\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Deploy Bifrost as a Docker Compose service on EC2 with provider API keys configured via environment variables, following established codebase patterns for containerization - Secure external access via Cloudflare Tunnel with Access service token authentication, ensuring only authenticated requests from Vercel can reach the gateway - Create verification scripts for both local and external connectivity testing to validate each acceptance criterion - Maintain all infrastructure configuration in a new `infrastructure/bifrost/` directory for version control and reproducibility
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: EC2 Instance Availability **Options Considered:** 1. Document EC2 provisioning as part of this plan 2. Assume EC2 exists and proceed with Bifrost deployment only **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** The ticket explicitly states "existing EC2 instance" and scopes this work to Bifrost deployment, tunnel setup, and verification only. EC2 provisioning appears to be a separate concern.
Design Choice 2: Infrastructure Files Location **Options Considered:** 1. Create files in `infrastructure/bifrost/` directory 2. Create files in `deployments/bifrost/` directory 3. Keep infrastructure separate from the application codebase **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Create a new `infrastructure/bifrost/` directory to establish a pattern for future infrastructure configurations while keeping deployment artifacts version-controlled.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Bifrost Docker Deployment with Provider Configuration This phase establishes the Bifrost gateway running locally on EC2 with both AI providers configured. The focus is on creating a production-ready Docker deployment that auto-restarts on failure and persists configuration across container restarts.
Task 1: Create Docker Compose Configuration Set up the Docker Compose file that defines the Bifrost service with appropriate restart policies, port bindings, and volume mounts. - Create `infrastructure/bifrost/docker-compose.yml` with the Bifrost service definition - Use the official `maximhq/bifrost` Docker image - Configure restart policy as `unless-stopped` or `always` to ensure auto-restart on failure - Bind port 8080 on localhost only (`127.0.0.1:8080:8080`) since Cloudflare Tunnel will handle external exposure - Mount a data volume for persistent configuration storage (config.json, config.db, logs.db) - Set appropriate resource limits (memory, CPU) based on expected load
Task 2: Configure AI Provider Environment Create the environment configuration for OpenAI and Anthropic providers that Bifrost will use to route requests. - Create `infrastructure/bifrost/.env.example` template documenting required environment variables - Configure `OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variable for OpenAI provider - Configure `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable for Anthropic provider - Set `APP_PORT=8080`, `APP_HOST=0.0.0.0`, and `LOG_LEVEL=info` for Bifrost runtime configuration - Document the provider routing pattern: `openai/` and `anthropic/` prefixes
Task 3: Create Bifrost Configuration File Set up the Bifrost gateway configuration for provider endpoints and basic routing. - Create `infrastructure/bifrost/config/config.json` with provider configurations - Configure OpenAI provider endpoint pointing to `https://api.openai.com/v1` - Configure Anthropic provider endpoint pointing to `https://api.anthropic.com/v1` - Reference API keys from environment using `env.OPENAI_API_KEY` and `env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` patterns - Ensure configuration enables the unified OpenAI-compatible endpoint at `/v1/chat/completions`
Task 4: Local Deployment Verification Script Create a script to verify the Bifrost deployment is functioning correctly before exposing it externally. - Create `infrastructure/bifrost/scripts/verify-local.sh` deployment verification script - Include health check verification against `localhost:8080/health` - Include test request to OpenAI provider via `openai/gpt-4o-mini` model prefix - Include test request to Anthropic provider via `anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet` model prefix - Output clear success/failure messages for each verification step
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase establishes the Bifrost gateway running locally on EC2 with both AI providers configured, creating a production-ready Docker deployment that auto-restarts on failure and persists configuration. **Docker Compose Setup:** - Create `infrastructure/bifrost/docker-compose.yml` defining the Bifrost service - Use the official `maximhq/bifrost` Docker image - Set restart policy to `unless-stopped` or `always` for automatic recovery - Bind port 8080 to localhost only (`127.0.0.1:8080:8080`) since Cloudflare Tunnel handles external exposure - Mount a data volume for persistent storage of config.json, config.db, and logs.db - Configure appropriate resource limits (memory, CPU) for expected load **Environment Configuration:** - Create `infrastructure/bifrost/.env.example` template file - Define `OPENAI_API_KEY` for OpenAI provider authentication - Define `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` for Anthropic provider authentication - Set `APP_PORT=8080`, `APP_HOST=0.0.0.0`, and `LOG_LEVEL=info` for Bifrost runtime - Document provider routing pattern using `openai/` and `anthropic/` prefixes **Bifrost Configuration:** - Create `infrastructure/bifrost/config/config.json` with provider configurations - Config
